danger_signals.visual_form: split-screen display showing AI opponent position and distance
hills, curves, and complex terrain visualized in isometric perspective
danger_signals.meaning: environmental obstacles that damage vehicle or reduce speed

danger_signals.visual_form: limited-use items (oil slicks, mines) consumable during race

safety_signals.meaning: successful progression; financial security for upgrades

safety_signals.visual_form: improved engine, tires, shocks, armor stats
safety_signals.meaning: increased performance capability; reduced vulnerability to damage

safety_signals.visual_form: custom track editor allowing player-designed courses
safety_signals.meaning: environmental mastery; control over challenge parameters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: minimal visual upgrades to vehicle; basic cosmetic appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: reduced acceleration, handling, and damage resilience; forced to use lower-tier race tracks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: visible equipment improvements reflected in vehicle model or UI stats display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: improved competitive performance; access to higher-tier races with greater prize money

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: mandatory split-screen showing player and AI opponent views simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: real-time competitive pressure; dynamic obstacle deployment and tactical item usage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: high-resolution graphics mode creating bland, washed-out color palette with dithering effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: sparse background detail; limited visual feedback differentiation for world states

environmental_storytelling.example: three vehicle types (truck, sports car, formula car) with purely cosmetic differences communicate player identity choice without mechanical consequence
career mode tiers with increasing complexity (straight ovals to complex hills and curves) visualize escalating difficulty and world danger
entry fees, upgrade costs, and prize money create risk-versus-reward visual story: under-equipped racers competing in higher tiers communicate desperation or ambition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: position and speed visible in split-screen; proximity indicates threat level; behavior adapts to player vehicle upgrades
reduced performance visible in handling degradation and speed loss
oil slicks and mines create environmental hazards affecting opponent behavior and trajectory

notes: RPM Racing communicates world-state primarily through mechanical systems (upgrades, currency, track difficulty tiers) rather than narrative or faction elements. Visual communication is constrained by SNES high-resolution mode limitations, resulting in bland, sparse aesthetics with dithering. No faction, political, or narrative world-state communication is present in available documentation. The game's danger/safety signals emerge from competitive racing mechanics (opponent proximity, resource management) rather than environmental or visual drama. Split-screen dual-view is primary mechanism for communicating competitive threat state.
